1541	
1542	\section{The glued Grassmannian scheme}
1543	\label{sec:gr_glued}
1544	
1545	\begin{definition}
1546	
1547	\leanok
1548	[The Grassmannian glue datum]
1549	  \label{def:gr_the_glue_data}
1550	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.theGlueData}
1551	  \uses{def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_chart_overlap, def:gr_chart_incl,
1552	    def:gr_chart_transition, def:gr_chart_transition',
1553	    lem:gr_chartIncl_isOpenImmersion, lem:gr_chartIncl_self_isIso,
1554	    lem:gr_chartTransition_self, lem:gr_chartTransition'_fac,
1555	    lem:gr_chartTransition'_cocycle}
1556	  The \emph{Grassmannian glue datum} is the \(\mathtt{Scheme.GlueData}\) bundle
1557	  indexed by the set \(\{\, I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\} : \#(I) = d \,\}\) of
1558	  size-\(d\) subsets, assembling the data constructed above:
1559	  \begin{itemize}
1560	    \item objects \(U := U^I\), the affine charts (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart});
1561	    \item overlaps \(V := U^I_J\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_overlap});
1562	    \item inclusions \(f := \bigl(U^I_J \hookrightarrow U^I\bigr)\)
1563	      (\cref{def:gr_chart_incl}), which are open immersions
1564	      (\cref{lem:gr_chartIncl_isOpenImmersion}), with the self-inclusion an
1565	      isomorphism (the \(f_{\mathrm{id}}\) field,
1566	      \cref{lem:gr_chartIncl_self_isIso});
1567	    \item transitions \(t := t_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition}), with
1568	      \(t_{\mathrm{id}}\) field \cref{lem:gr_chartTransition_self};
1569	    \item the triple-overlap field \(t' := t'_{I,J,K}\)
1570	      (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition'}), with compatibility field
1571	      \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) (\cref{lem:gr_chartTransition'_fac}) and \(\mathrm{cocycle}\)
1572	      field (\cref{lem:gr_chartTransition'_cocycle}).
1573	  \end{itemize}
1574	  The remaining structural fields \(f_{\mathrm{mono}}\) (each inclusion is a
1575	  monomorphism) and \(f_{\mathrm{hasPullback}}\) (the relevant fibre products
1576	  exist) are discharged by instance synthesis from the open-immersion property of
1577	  the inclusions. Assembling these named fields into the
1578	  \(\mathtt{Scheme.GlueData}\) record yields the datum from which the glued scheme
1579	  (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) is formed.
1580	\end{definition}
1581	
1582	\begin{definition}
1583	
1584	\leanok
1585	[The Grassmannian scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over \(\mathbb{Z}\)]
1586	  \label{def:gr_glued_scheme}
1587	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scheme}
1588	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle,
1589	    def:gr_chart_overlap, def:gr_chart_incl, lem:gr_chartIncl_isOpenImmersion,
1590	    lem:gr_chartIncl_self_isIso, def:gr_chart_transition,
1591	    lem:gr_chartTransition_self, def:gr_away_pullback_iso,
1592	    lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_fst, lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_snd,
1593	    def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv, def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij,
1594	    def:gr_chart_transition', lem:gr_chartTransition'_fac,
1595	    lem:gr_chartTransition'_ringIdentity, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
1596	    lem:gr_cocycle_phi_id, lem:gr_chartTransition'_cocycle}
1597	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
1598	  % affine patches" (read from
1599	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L843-L850).
1600	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1601	  % "Therefore the schemes
1602	  %  $U^I$, as $I$ varies over all the
1603	  %  ${r\choose d}$ different
1604	  %  subsets of  $\{ 1,\ldots, r\}$ of cardinality $d$,
1605	  %  can be glued together by the co-cycle $(\theta_{I,J})$ to form a
1606	  %  finite-type scheme $\grass(r,d)$ over $\Z$. As each $U^I$ is
1607	  %  isomorphic to $\AA^{d(r-d)}_{\Z}$, it follows that
1608	  %  $\grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z$ is smooth of relative dimension $d(r-d)$."
1609	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
1610	  Gluing the affine charts \(\{U^I\}\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}), one for each
1611	  of the \(\binom{r}{d}\) subsets \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) of cardinality
1612	  \(d\), along the open subschemes \(U^I_J \subseteq U^I\) via the transition
1613	  isomorphisms \(\theta_{I,J} : U^I_J \xrightarrow{\sim} U^J_I\)
1614	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}) -- which satisfy the cocycle condition
1615	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{J,K} \circ \theta_{I,J}\) and \(\theta_{I,I} = \id\)
1616	  of \cref{lem:gr_cocycle} -- yields a scheme
1617	  \[
1618	    \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \longrightarrow \Spec \mathbb{Z}.
1619	  \]
1620	  It is of finite type over \(\mathbb{Z}\) (a finite gluing of finitely
1621	  generated \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebras), and under the gluing each \(U^I\) is an
1622	  open subscheme of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) with \(U^I \cap U^J = U^I_J\).
1623	
1624	  \emph{Construction of the glue datum.} The gluing is carried out through the
1625	  Mathlib vehicle for scheme gluing, a glue datum in the sense of a category of
1626	  schemes (the structure \(\mathtt{Scheme.GlueData}\), extending the abstract
1627	  glue-datum interface). Its data are supplied as follows. The index set is the
1628	  set \(J := \{\, I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\} : \#(I) = d \,\}\) of size-\(d\)
1629	  subsets. The objects are the charts \(U^I\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}); the
1630	  pairwise overlaps are the schemes \(U^I_J\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_overlap}); the
1631	  inclusions \(f_{I,J} : U^I_J \to U^I\) are the open immersions
1632	  \(\cref{def:gr_chart_incl}\) (open immersions by
1633	  \cref{lem:gr_chartIncl_isOpenImmersion}, in particular monomorphisms), with the
1634	  identity field \(f_{\mathrm{id}}\) supplied by
1635	  \cref{lem:gr_chartIncl_self_isIso}; the transition isomorphisms
1636	  \(t_{I,J} : U^I_J \to U^J_I\) are the scheme-level transitions
1637	  \(\cref{def:gr_chart_transition}\), with identity field \(t_{\mathrm{id}}\)
1638	  supplied by \cref{lem:gr_chartTransition_self}.
1639	
1640	  The triple-overlap field \(t'\) and its first coherence obligation are supplied
1641	  by the two preceding results. The field itself is the morphism
1642	  \(t'_{I,J,K} : U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K \to U^J_K \times_{U^J} U^J_I\) of
1643	  \cref{def:gr_chart_transition'}, and the compatibility field
1644	  \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) -- that \(t'\) is compatible with the projections to the
1645	  charts -- is \cref{lem:gr_chartTransition'_fac}, which reduces (both fibre
1646	  products being affine) to the ring identity
1647	  \cref{lem:gr_chartTransition'_ringIdentity} via the leg lemmas
1648	  \cref{lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_fst} and \cref{lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_snd}.
1649	
1650	  \emph{Cocycle field.} The \(t'\)-cocycle coherence
1651	  \(t'_{I,J,K} \circ t'_{J,K,I} \circ t'_{K,I,J} = \mathrm{id}\)
1652	  follows from \cref{lem:gr_chartTransition'_cocycle} via the ring identity
1653	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle_phi_id}. With this field in hand the glue datum is
1654	  complete: it is the structure \(\mathtt{Scheme.GlueData}\) with
1655	  \(U := \texttt{affineChart}\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}),
1656	  \(V := \texttt{chartOverlap}\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_overlap}),
1657	  \(f := \texttt{chartIncl}\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_incl}),
1658	  \(t := \texttt{chartTransition}\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition}),
1659	  \(t_{\mathrm{id}} := \texttt{chartTransition\_self}\)
1660	  (\cref{lem:gr_chartTransition_self}),
1661	  \(t' := \texttt{chartTransition'}\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition'}),
1662	  \(t_{\mathrm{fac}} := \texttt{chartTransition'\_fac}\)
1663	  (\cref{lem:gr_chartTransition'_fac}), and the cocycle field above. The glued
1664	  scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is then the colimit
1665	  \((\text{glue datum}).\mathrm{glued}\) produced by the gluing machine.
1666	
1667	  Moreover \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) is \emph{smooth of relative
1668	  dimension \(d(r-d)\)}. Smoothness and relative dimension are local on the
1669	  source; the charts \(\{U^I\}\) cover \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), and each
1670	  \(U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) is
1671	  affine space of dimension \(d(r-d)\) over \(\mathbb{Z}\), which is smooth over
1672	  \(\mathbb{Z}\) of relative dimension \(d(r-d)\) (the polynomial ring
1673	  \(\mathbb{Z}[x_1, \dots, x_{d(r-d)}]\) is a smooth \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra). As
1674	  the property may be checked on an open cover of the source, the conclusion
1675	  follows.
1676	\end{definition}
1677	
1678	\section{Separatedness}
1679	\label{sec:gr_separated}
1680	
1681	The surjectivity of the restricted-diagonal comorphism is the ring-theoretic
1682	heart of separatedness. We isolate it through the following sequence of
1683	helper results before stating the keystone lemma.
1684	
1685	\begin{lemma}
1686	
1687	\leanok
1688	[Two-sided inverse identity \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)\,P^I_J = 1\)]
1689	  \label{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul}
1690	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul}
1691	  \uses{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, lem:gr_transition_pre_unit, lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet}
1692	  In \(R^I_J = \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\), the pre-localisation transition hom
1693	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}) satisfies the explicit
1694	  two-sided inverse identity
1695	  \[
1696	    \tilde\theta_{I,J}\bigl(P^J_I\bigr) \cdot P^I_J \;=\; 1 .
1697	  \]
1698	  This refines the unit assertion of \cref{lem:gr_transition_pre_unit} to a named
1699	  inverse: \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)\) is the explicit inverse of \(P^I_J\) in
1700	  \(R^I_J\).
1701	\end{lemma}
1702	\begin{proof}
1703	  \uses{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, lem:gr_transition_pre_unit, lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet}
1704	  \leanok
1705	  By \cref{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet} (taking the third subset \(K = I\)),
1706	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)\) is the determinant of the \(I\)-minor of the image
1707	  matrix \(M = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\), which by
1708	  \cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap} equals
1709	  \(\det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I\bigr) = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr)\). Hence
1710	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = \det(X^I_J)^{-1}\), and multiplying by \(P^I_J =
1711	  \det(X^I_J)\) gives \(\det(X^I_J)^{-1} \cdot \det(X^I_J) = 1\) by the
1712	  localisation-inverse cancellation for the unit \(\det(X^I_J)\)
1713	  (\cref{lem:gr_transition_pre_unit}).
1714	\end{proof}
1715	
1716	\begin{definition}
1717	
1718	\leanok
1719	[Restricted-diagonal comorphism \(\delta_{I,J}\)]
1720	  \label{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}
1721	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.diagonalRingMap}
1722	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre}
1723	  The \emph{restricted-diagonal comorphism} on the patch \(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}}
1724	  U^J\) is the \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism
1725	  \[

1803	\begin{proof}
1804	  \uses{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_left, lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_right, lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul}
1805	  \leanok
1806	  Let \(z \in R^I_J\). Since \(R^I_J = \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\) is the
1807	  localisation of \(R^I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) away from \(P^I_J\), surjectivity of
1808	  the localisation map provides \(a \in R^I\) and \(n \in \mathbb{N}\) with
1809	  \[
1810	    z \cdot (P^I_J)^n \;=\; \mathrm{alg}_{R^I \to R^I_J}(a).
1811	  \]
1812	  Consider the witness \(a \otimes (P^J_I)^n \in \mathbb{Z}[X^I]
1813	  \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathbb{Z}[X^J]\). Using multiplicativity together with
1814	  \cref{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_left} and \cref{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_right},
1815	  \[

1817	      = \mathrm{alg}_{R^I \to R^I_J}(a) \cdot \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)^n
1818	      = z \cdot (P^I_J)^n \cdot \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)^n
1819	      = z \cdot \bigl(P^I_J \cdot \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)\bigr)^n
1820	      = z,
1821	  \]
1822	  where the last equality uses \(P^I_J \cdot \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = 1\) from
1823	  \cref{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul}. Hence \(z\) lies in the image
1824	  of \(\delta_{I,J}\), so \(\delta_{I,J}\) is surjective.

2016	\begin{lemma}
2017	
2018	\leanok
2019	[\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is separated over \(\mathbb{Z}\)]
2020	  \label{lem:gr_separated}
2021	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isSeparated}
2022	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_surjective, lem:gr_separated_toSpecZ}
2023	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Separatedness" (read from
2024	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L856-L860).
2025	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2026	  % "The intersection of the diagonal of $\grass(r,d)$ with $U^I\times U^J$
2027	  %  can be seen to be the closed subscheme $\Delta_{I,J}\subset U^I\times U^J$
2028	  %  defined by entries of the matrix formula $X^J_I\,X^I - X^J =0$,
2029	  %  and so $\grass(r,d)$ is a separated scheme."
2030	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
2031	  The structure morphism \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\)
2032	  (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) is separated; equivalently, the diagonal
2033	  \(\Delta : \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \times_{\mathbb{Z}}
2034	  \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is a closed immersion.
2035	\end{lemma}
2036	
2037	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The intersection of the diagonal of $\grass(r,d)$ with
2038	% $U^I\times U^J$ can be seen to be the closed subscheme
2039	% $\Delta_{I,J}\subset U^I\times U^J$ defined by entries of the matrix formula
2040	% $X^J_I\,X^I - X^J =0$, and so $\grass(r,d)$ is a separated scheme."
2041	% (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L856-L860.)
2042	\begin{proof}
2043	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_surjective, lem:gr_separated_toSpecZ}
2044	  \leanok
2045	  The products \(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J\), as \(I, J\) range over the
2046	  size-\(d\) subsets, form an affine open cover of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)
2047	  \times_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\). Separatedness may be checked on this
2048	  cover: it suffices to show that for each pair \((I,J)\) the preimage
2049	  \(\Delta^{-1}(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J)\) maps by a closed immersion into
2050	  \(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J\), i.e.\ that the corresponding ring
2051	  homomorphism is surjective.
2052	
2053	  By \cref{def:gr_glued_scheme} the diagonal preimage is the overlap
2054	  \(\Delta^{-1}(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J) = U^I \cap U^J = U^I_J = \Spec
2055	  \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\). The restricted diagonal corresponds to the ring map
2056	  \[
2057	    \delta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathbb{Z}[X^J]
2058	    \longrightarrow \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J],
2059	    \qquad
2060	    X^I \otimes 1 \mapsto X^I, \quad 1 \otimes X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I,
2061	  \]
2062	  the second component being the comorphism \(\theta_{I,J}\) of
2063	  \cref{def:gr_transition} (a point of \(U^I_J\) and its image under
2064	  \(\theta_{I,J}\) are the two coordinates of a point of the diagonal). We claim
2065	  \(\delta_{I,J}\) is surjective. Its image contains all entries of \(X^I\)
2066	  (from the first factor). From the second factor it contains the entries of
2067	  \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\); taking the \(I\)-minor of this matrix, and using
2068	  \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\), the image contains the entries of \((X^I_J)^{-1}\),
2069	  and in particular
2070	  \[
2071	    \delta_{I,J}(1 \otimes P^J_I) = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr) = 1/P^I_J .
2072	  \]
2073	  Hence \(1/P^I_J\) lies in the image, so the image is all of
2074	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\) and \(\delta_{I,J}\) is surjective. Concretely the
2075	  closed subscheme \(\Delta_{I,J} \subseteq U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J\) is cut
2076	  out by the entries of the matrix relation
2077	  \[
2078	    X^J_I \, X^I - X^J \;=\; 0,
2079	  \]
2080	  which on the diagonal holds because \(X^J = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) forces
2081	  \(X^J_I = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) and therefore \(X^J_I X^I =
2082	  (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I = X^J\). As each restricted diagonal is a closed immersion,
2083	  \(\Delta\) is a closed immersion and \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is separated over
2084	  \(\mathbb{Z}\).
2085	\end{proof}

2087	\section{Properness}
2088	\label{sec:gr_proper}
2089	
2090	Following Nitsure \S 1, the structure morphism \(\pi : \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec
2091	\mathbb{Z}\) is proper. We reduce properness to the \emph{existence} half of the
2092	valuative criterion: the Mathlib criterion \texttt{IsProper.of\_valuativeCriterion}
2093	reduces \(\mathrm{IsProper}\,\pi\) to four ingredients --- quasi-compactness,
2094	quasi-separatedness, local finiteness of type, and the valuative criterion
2095	\(\mathrm{Existence} \sqcap \mathrm{Uniqueness}\). The first three and the
2096	uniqueness half are cheap (uniqueness is free from separatedness), so the only
2097	genuine content is \(\mathrm{Existence}\): every commutative square from a
2098	valuation ring admits a diagonal filler. This existence statement is the Nitsure
2099	chart-selection argument, which we decompose into four steps E1--E4.
2100	
2101	\subsection*{Cheap ingredients and the reduction to existence}
